claim_evidence_links.claim_text: Jeff is described as a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone.
The system is strongest in the areas of research support, anti-drift coverage, truthful inspection, bounded `/run`, and approval-gated continuation.
Jeff is explicitly not a broad API surface, a GUI, a broad memory product, or an autonomous continuation system.

Jeff is described as a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone.
evidence_items.evidence_text: Jeff is a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone. It is strongest today at truthful inspection, bounded `/run`, approval-gated continuation, research support, and anti-drift coverage. It is not a GUI, a broad API surface, an autonomous continuation system, or a broad memory product.

evidence_items.claim: Jeff is described as a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone.
Jeff is described as a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone.
evidence_items.evidence_text: After `pip install -e .` the `jeff` console script is also available as a bare command; without the install, use `python -m jeff`.

evidence_items.claim: Jeff is described as a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone.
evidence_items.evidence_text: Startup loads or initializes a persisted local runtime workspace under `.jeff_runtime`. On a fresh or reset runtime, Jeff initializes `project-1` / `wu-1` with no seeded run history; later startups reload the persisted canonical state and support records already present in that workspace. No daemon, GUI, or background continuation is started.

evidence_items.claim: Jeff is described as a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone.
evidence_items.evidence_text: A local `jeff.runtime.toml` enables the bounded repo-local validation `/run <repo-local-validation-objective>` path and the research commands. Without it, the read-oriented CLI still works, but `/run <repo-local-validation-objective>` and `/research ...` remain unavailable.

evidence_items.claim: Jeff is described as a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone.
evidence_items.evidence_text: ```text python -m jeff --help python -m jeff --bootstrap-check python -m jeff --reset-runtime --bootstrap-check ```

evidence_items.claim: Jeff is described as a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone.
evidence_items.evidence_text: ```text python -m jeff --command "/help" python -m jeff --command "/project list" python -m jeff --project project-1 --work wu-1 --command "/run list" --json ```

Jeff is described as a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone.
evidence_items.evidence_text: The primary implemented operator surface is the CLI under `jeff.interface`.

evidence_items.claim: Jeff is described as a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone.
evidence_items.evidence_text: - `/run <repo-local-validation-objective>` is the current bounded repo-local validation entry when `jeff.runtime.toml` is loaded. It drives one fixed smoke pytest validation plan with captured command evidence; it is not a general command runner. - `approve` records a bound approval, `revalidate` continues or fails closed, and `reject` terminally blocks that continuation for the current bounded ru

findings: Jeff is described as a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone.
The system is strongest in the areas of research support, anti-drift coverage, truthful inspection, bounded `/run`, and approval-gated continuation.
Jeff is explicitly not a broad API surface, a GUI, a broad memory product, or an autonomous continuation system.
inference: JEFF aims to provide a controlled, persisted environment for runtime validation and inspection rather than serving as a general-purpose autonomous agent or user interface.
question_or_objective: what is the goal of JEFF
summary: Evidence extracted for this bounded research output remains inspectable with provenance preserved.
title: Evidence bundle: what is the goal of JEFF
uncertainty: No explicit uncertainties identified from the provided evidence.
